resources.string: Enable Snowflake. Help others bypass censorship.
Snowflake is Active. You’re helping bypass censorship.
Please disable battery optimizations for this app so it can run in the background successfully

Snowflake allows people to connect to the Tor network in places where Tor is blocked by routing your connection through volunteer proxies located in uncensored countries. \n\nSimilar to VPNs, which help users bypass Internet censorship, Snowflake disguises your Internet activity as though they’re making a video or voice call, making you less detectable to Internet censors.

Snowflake uses a technology called WebRTC, which is commonly employed by videoconferencing software. This helps mask your use of Tor from censors by making it appear as though you’re on an audio or video call instead. \n\nLearn more at https://snowflake.torproject.org

There is no need to worry about which websites people are accessing through your Snowflake app. Their visible browsing IP address will match their Tor exit node, not yours (since you’re an entrypoint).
Can I circumvent censorship using this app?
No. If you want to circumvent censorship, you need to download a Tor-powered app such as Tor Browser or Orbot and select Snowflake on the app’s settings.
This app was made by Bloco, an Android studio based in Portugal. It was built upon great work by the Tor and Guardian Project teams.
We’ll ask Android to start the app in the background
Only run if connected to an unmetered network like WiFi
Only run if the device battery is charging
How many people to help at the same time

plurals.name: settings_capacity_specific
plurals.item: Help %d person at the same time
Help %d persons at the same time

resources.string: Help an unlimited number of persons
Your Snowflake is ready to help users circumvent censorship.
Your Snowflake is currently helping someone circumvent censorship.
